title: Scattering Amplitudes, Multi-variate Residues and Valuated Matroids
speaker: Nick Early
abstract: Multi-variate residues on Grassmannians G(k,n) and moduli spaces M0,n are ubiquitous in the study of scattering amplitudes; they provide a powerful and essential tool. Amenable theories include the biadjoint scalar, NLSM, Yang-Mills, gravity and N=4 Super Yang-Mills and more. I will review a relation between such multi-variate residues and the real tropical Grassmannian, i.e. the set of valuated matroids which are realizable over the real numbers. In this way, a physical problem becomes a question in geometric combinatorics.

proof_stages.label: Kinematic and moduli-space setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up massless momenta, Mandelstam invariants, tree-level cubic scalar Feynman sums, the kinematic space, and the identification X_{2,n}=M_{0,n} as the basic configuration space.

proof_stages.label: CHY formula as a residue on M_{0,n}
proof_stages.summary: Parke-Taylor factors and the scattering potential are introduced, and the Cachazo-He-Yuan critical-point sum is explained as a multivariate residue computing the same amplitudes as the Feynman expansion.

proof_stages.label: Planar basis and minimal kinematics
proof_stages.summary: The planar pole coordinates x_{ij} and cross ratios are organized into a basis, and the minimal-kinematics specialization reduces the amplitude to a simple formula explained combinatorially by triangulations of a root polytope.

proof_stages.label: Oriented-matroid tiles and decoupling identities
proof_stages.summary: Real Grassmannian/configuration-space tiles are treated via oriented matroids, each carrying a logarithmic differential form, and this viewpoint yields Parke-Taylor decoupling identities and their more intricate X_{3,6} analogues.

proof_stages.label: Generalized biadjoint amplitudes on X_{k,n}
proof_stages.summary: Using Grassmannian duality as motivation, the CHY construction is generalized to X_{k,n}, leading to generalized Parke-Taylor amplitudes and a correspondence with maximal cones of the tropical Grassmannian.

proof_stages.label: Hypersimplex planar basis and tropical Plucker relations
proof_stages.summary: A higher-k analogue of the planar basis is constructed from directed distances in the hypersimplex, producing weight vectors satisfying the positive tropical Plucker relations characteristic of the positive tropical Grassmannian.

proof_stages.label: Compatibility theorem for generalized poles
proof_stages.summary: The generalized noncrossing condition is stated: two k-subsets are compatible precisely when their difference alternates sign exactly twice in cyclic order, with proof reduced to checking octahedral faces and induced matroid subdivisions.

examples.name: The X_{3,6} tropical bipyramid term
examples.note: The explicit X_{3,6} generalized amplitude term corresponding to a bipyramid cone exposes new non-cyclic poles such as s_{246}, motivating the search for a generalized Feynman-diagram and compatibility combinatorics.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker presents the X_{3,6} tropical Grassmannian bipyramid contribution and discusses its pole structure and nontrivial linear relations among the factors

examples.name: Crossing diagonals in a square
examples.note: The four-point square, where the product of the two crossing chords is forbidden, serves as the familiar model for the generalized noncrossing condition among higher k-subset poles.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies the square triangulation/crossing rule concretely, explaining why the product x13 and x24 is forbidden because the two diagonals cross

passes.reason: the crossing diagonals x13 and x24 are used as the forbidden incompatible pair showing that the product cannot occur in a planar triangulation.
The crossing square diagonals are used as the forbidden obstruction to a triangulation term such as 1/(x13 x24).
The square with both diagonals is the minimal symmetric crossing obstruction to a planar triangulation, used to rule out the product 1/(x_{13}x_{24}).
the crossing diagonals are used as the forbidden obstruction to a square triangulation/product term
the two diagonals give the D4-symmetric minimal incompatible chord configuration, used to rule out the would-be term x_{13}^{-1}x_{24}^{-1}.
